page: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance type built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression are categorized in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, passmark CPU scores, and extended instruction tests fall into the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests across both small and large blocks show strong performance in the top 25%, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4504.62 GB/sec. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 25%, while LLM inference speeds range from average to poor, with 7B model text generation falling into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5d.24xlarge offers a high-density memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 combined with large local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput workloads. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core performance relative to its strong multi-threaded and memory bandwidth capabilities. This profile makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from local NVMe instance storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, passmark CPU, and memory bandwidth tests all ranking in the top 25%. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are also highly rated. Conversely, LLM text generation performance is weak to poor. This resource density and high memory-to-vCPU ratio make the instance suitable for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput multi-threaded workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage, dense DDR4 RAM, and strong multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 1:8 resource ratio
3600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded processing
